ds.add.command.copy-unavailable: Clipboard access is unavailable. Copy the command from the box below.
ds.add.command.help: Paste it into PowerShell on the PC you want to control. It includes this server's Site token.
ds.add.command.title: Register another PC by copy-paste
ds.add.selfhost.help: This PC is normally registered automatically. Use this form to add another PC over a private LAN, VPN, or Tailscale address. The site checks the daemon before saving it.
ds.add.selfhost.token.help: For another PC, paste the connector daemon token from that PC. The same PC you started DeskRelay on is usually already registered.
dsd.approval.clear.confirm: Clear all auto-approved tools for this device?
dsd.approval.help: claude tools you've passed with "Always allow" on this device. Stored only in your browser's localStorage — never sent to the server.
dsd.cwd.help: Each new chat opens with this path. Leave empty to fall back to C:\Users\.
dsd.danger.help: Removing this device deletes it from this browser.
dsd.repair.help: Re-pair this device without losing its identity. Chats, sessions, and per-device settings are preserved. If the daemon is already running on this PC it will pick up the new credentials automatically; otherwise run the printed command on that PC.
dsd.repair.instructions: Run this command on the paired PC. If a daemon is already running there, it will adopt the new credentials as soon as the code is consumed.
dsd.security-profile.help: Controls what happens when the connector daemon is unreachable mid-chat. Pick the profile that matches this device.
dsd.security-profile.normal.note: Normal: when the daemon cannot be reached, tool calls are blocked. Recommended default for remote use.
dsd.security-profile.relaxed.note: Relaxed: when the daemon can't be reached, tool calls are allowed through. OK for trusted personal workstations.
dsd.security-profile.strict.note: Strict: same as Normal today; reserved for stronger future enforcement (manifest permission policy, sandboxing).
dsd.unpair.suggest-repair: Removing "{label}" deletes its per-device settings from this browser. Continue?
dw.behaviors.empty: No behaviors loaded on this device.
dw.behaviors.path.placeholder: absolute path to behavior package (manifest.json + entry)
landing.feature1.body: Files, shell access, git state, MCP config, and Anthropic credentials stay on the PC running the connector.
landing.feature1.title: Your machines stay yours
landing.feature2.body: This PC is registered automatically. Add other PCs over LAN, VPN, or Tailscale only when you need them.
landing.feature2.title: Several devices
landing.feature3.body: Continue sessions, approve tool calls, preview generated images, and start new Claude Code runs remotely.
landing.feature3.title: Browser control
landing.footer.disclaimer: Do not expose connector ports directly to the public internet.
landing.headline:
DeskRelay
for your own PCs
landing.how.step1: Start **DeskRelay** on this PC. Its connector daemon is registered automatically.
landing.how.step2: On this PC, click **Open app** to enter without typing the Site token. From another device, open the printed URL and paste the **Site token**.
landing.how.step3: To use another PC, run the connector there and add its daemon URL + token in **Settings -> Devices**.
landing.how.title: Self-host in three steps
landing.signin.title: Enter Site token
landing.subhead: Start DeskRelay on this PC, use its Claude Code immediately, and add other PCs only when you need them.
legal.privacy.title: Privacy for self-hosted DeskRelay
legal.privacy.intro: DeskRelay self is open-source software that you run on your own computers. It is not a hosted service, app-store package, central relay, payment product, or managed account service.
legal.privacy.section.1.title: What the project maintainers receive
legal.privacy.section.1.body: The DeskRelay maintainers do not receive, store, or process your chats, prompts, Claude responses, files, command output, device list, Site token, daemon tokens, Claude session files, or connector logs from a normal self-host installation.
legal.privacy.section.2.title: Where data stays
legal.privacy.section.2.body: Device registration state stays on the machine where you run the site backend. The browser stores the Site token and UI preferences locally. The connector daemon stores its token and state on the PC where it runs. Claude Code session files, command output, files, git state, MCP config, and generated command files stay on your own PCs.
legal.privacy.section.3.title: Third-party services
legal.privacy.section.3.body: Claude Code and any AI provider, package manager, Git host, VPN, SSH provider, operating system, or network service you choose to use may handle data under their own policies. DeskRelay does not change those terms.
legal.privacy.section.4.title: Your responsibilities
legal.privacy.section.4.body: Keep Site tokens, daemon tokens, SSH keys, and generated command files private. Use LAN, SSH, or a private VPN such as Tailscale instead of exposing ports to the public internet. Set workspace roots before remote use if you want to limit which folders Claude Code can reach.
legal.privacy.foot: For issues or corrections, use the project repository issue tracker.
legal.terms.title: Terms for self-hosted DeskRelay
legal.terms.intro: DeskRelay self is open-source software that you install and operate yourself. The project maintainers do not provide a hosted service, uptime guarantee, account system, paid subscription, app-store distribution, central relay, or managed support contract for this self-host version.
legal.terms.section.1.title: Use only systems you are allowed to control
legal.terms.section.1.body: DeskRelay can route prompts to Claude Code and expose file, shell, git, MCP, and tool-use surfaces on the connector PC. Use it only on computers, accounts, networks, and projects that you own or are authorized to operate.
legal.terms.section.2.title: Security and operations
legal.terms.section.2.body: You are responsible for access control, network exposure, backups, logs, and local tokens. Do not expose connector or site ports directly to the public internet. Review Claude Code tool calls carefully before approving them. Commands run through Claude Code affect the connector PC, not a DeskRelay-managed sandbox.
legal.terms.section.3.title: Third-party software
legal.terms.section.3.body: Claude Code, Anthropic services, Bun, Git, package registries, Tailscale, SSH software, and your operating system are separate products with their own terms, licenses, and security models. You are responsible for following them.
legal.terms.section.4.title: License and warranty
legal.terms.section.4.body: DeskRelay is provided under the licenses in this repository. The software is provided as-is, without warranty. To the maximum extent permitted by law, the maintainers are not liable for data loss, command execution, service interruption, security incidents, or other damages arising from your self-host operation.
legal.terms.foot: The repository license remains the controlling software license.
locale-chooser.subtitle: Pick the language you want to read in. You can change it later in Settings.
login.error.allowlist_denied.body: This server rejected the access attempt ({detail}). Check the current Site token.
login.error.allowlist_denied.title: Access not allowed
login.error.auth_failed.body: Something went wrong during sign-in ({detail}). Please try again.
login.error.auth_failed.title: Sign-in failed
login.error.contact: Copy the current Site token from the server terminal or status command.
login.error.csrf.body: The sign-in flow lost track of itself ({detail}). This usually means the link was opened from a stale tab. Please try again.
login.error.csrf.title: Sign-in expired
login.error.email_unverified.body: Your provider account doesn't have a verified email yet ({detail}). Verify your email with the provider, then try again.
login.error.email_unverified.title: Email not verified
login.error.provider_denied.body: The optional identity provider denied sign-in ({detail}). Use the Site token printed by this DeskRelay server.
login.error.provider_denied.title: Provider declined sign-in
login.error.provider_misconfigured.body: The optional identity provider is misconfigured ({detail}). Use the Site token or fix the provider settings.
login.error.provider_misconfigured.title: Provider misconfigured
login.no-providers: No SSO providers are configured. Use the Site token printed by this DeskRelay server.
login.title: Site token
login.token.help: Paste the Site token printed by the server start script.
login.token.invalid: That Site token was rejected. Copy the current Site token from the server terminal or status command.
